title: Type Constraint
description: Base shape for a constraint entry that selects one branch of a typed/discriminated union. The `type` value identifies the branch, and `constraints` applies to the selected branch's schema. Domain-specific schemas extend this base to document what the `type` refers to and to narrow `constraints` to the selected branch's constraint schema.

type.description: The discriminator value for the constrained branch. Concrete schemas typically narrow this with `const`, `enum`, or another type-family constraint.

constraints.description: Constraints applied to the branch selected by `type`. Concrete typed-constraint schemas should narrow this to the selected branch's constraint schema.
